text: L-Alanine, chemical formula C3H7NO2, molecular weight 89.09, is a single-component organic amino-acid molecular crystal. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, non-polar (enantiomorphic) orthorhombic space group P2_1 2_1 2_1 (No. 19, point group 222), with unit cell a = 6.032 Å, b = 12.343 Å, c = 5.784 Å, Z = 4. Zwitterionic alanine molecules are linked by a three-dimensional network of hydrogen bonds. Because the 222 point group is non-polar, L-alanine is piezoelectric but not pyroelectric or ferroelectric; its only non-zero piezoelectric coefficients (d14, d25, d36) are shear terms, so a piezoelectric response is activated solely by shear strain. In this work, L-alanine nanocrystals grown inside anodized-alumina nanopores with preferred [001] orientation produced a piezoelectric current that detected mechanical pressures as low as about 3 Pa at atmospheric ambient, with current amplitude increasing linearly with applied pressure; the sensitivity is attributed to easily deformed hydrogen bonds. No numerical piezoelectric coefficient was reported. The crystal structure (CCDC 1203201, CSD refcode LALNIN01) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 6.1% and a density of 1.374 g/cm3. The piezoelectric pressure-sensing study, which also covered beta-glycine nanocrystals, was reported by Bishara et al. in Materials Science & Engineering C (2020).
property_ref_title: Amino acids nanocrystals for piezoelectric detection of ultra-low mechanical pressure
